pages.title: First steps
pages.text: Craft a wooden stave, press $(l)G$() to open the self-casting circle, then write $(l)心$() to pad your health.$(br2)Before looking for a fight, write $(l)命$() for one cheap death save. Before entering caves, try $(l)明$() or $(l)脉$() to check for danger first.

pages.title: Self-casting circle
pages.text: $(l)G$() opens the self-casting circle. Glyphs written here attach to you.$(br)Hold left click to draw, then release to recognize; you can also press $(l)Enter$() to recognize manually.$(br2)Most body glyphs use this circle. You can also write $(l)泉$() for an instant heal or $(l)墨$() to unfold an ink field.

pages.title: Inscription circle
pages.text: $(l)V$() opens the inscription circle. Glyphs written here anchor to the world instead of your body.$(br2)Use it to write $(l)泉$() on a block for nearby healing, or write $(l)山$() and $(l)裂$() to shape temporary terrain. $(l)印$() first asks you to pick a block, then reopens the circle for the second glyph.$(br2)Either circle closes if you move 10 blocks away, so it cannot keep eating left clicks after a teleport.

pages.title: Three rules
pages.text: The glyph decides the intent: $(l)心$() means health, $(l)怒$() means getting hit and striking back.$(br2)The stave decides the tier: the same glyph becomes stronger, lasts longer, or gains extra rules on better staves. If your stave is too weak, the game tells you.$(br2)Most custom effects expire. Death, leaving the world, or switching saves clears them.

pages.title: If it fails
pages.text: If recognition is wrong, write again. If a glyph does nothing, check three things:$(br)1. Are you holding a stave?$(br)2. Is the stave tier high enough?$(br)3. Did you move more than 10 blocks from the circle?$(br2)Do not force long glyphs while surrounded. Stabilize first with short survival glyphs like $(l)心$(), $(l)命$(), and $(l)忍$().
